questions.question: In an Insert operation in a Linked List, what happens to the existing node's next pointer when a new node is inserted at the beginning of the list?
options: A) It becomes null
B) It remains the same
C) It becomes the new node
D) It becomes the previous node
questions.explanation: When a new node is inserted at the beginning of the list, the existing node's next pointer becomes the new node.

questions.question: In a Delete operation in a Linked List, what happens to the memory occupied by the deleted node?
options: A) It is freed immediately
B) It is freed later when garbage collection happens
C) It is not freed and the next pointer becomes null
D) It is not freed and the previous node's next pointer becomes the deleted node
questions.explanation: In a Delete operation in a Linked List, the memory occupied by the deleted node is not freed and the next pointer becomes null.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the Display operation in a Linked List?
options: A) To delete a node
B) To insert a node
C) To traverse the list and display its elements
D) To insert a node at the beginning of the list
questions.explanation: The primary purpose of the Display operation in a Linked List is to traverse the list and display its elements.

questions.question: In an Insert operation in a Linked List, what happens to the next pointer of the new node when it is inserted at the end of the list?
options: A) It becomes null
B) It remains the same
C) It becomes the previous node
D) It becomes the next node
questions.explanation: When a new node is inserted at the end of the list, the next pointer of the new node becomes the next node in the list.

questions.question: In a Delete operation in a Linked List, what happens to the node's data after it is deleted?
options: A) It is copied to the previous node
B) It is copied to the next node
C) It is not copied and the next pointer becomes null
D) It is not copied and the previous node's next pointer becomes the deleted node
questions.explanation: In a Delete operation in a Linked List, the node's data is not copied and the next pointer becomes null.
